perturbation.original_text: "Product" means the FDA-approved oral formulation of TPOXX® (tecovirimat) for use in the Field, or any oral formulation of tecovirimat for use in the Field for which a Regulatory Authority grants Drug Approval to SIGA in a country or jurisdiction in the Territory.
perturbation.changed_text: "Product" means the FDA-approved oral formulation of TPOXX® (tecovirimat) for use in the Field.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "or any oral formulation of tecovirimat for use in the Field for which a Regulatory Authority grants Drug Approval to SIGA in a country or jurisdiction in the Territory." The definition of 'Product' is now solely tied to the FDA-approved oral formulation of TPOXX. Later in Section 7.3, reference is made to seeking trademark registrations for the SIGA Trademarks. This section now creates an in-text contradiction: Section 7.3 refers to trademark registrations for products approved by Governmental Authorities, which the definition of 'Product' (post-perturbation) now explicitly excludes, thus making it contradictory and making it difficult to enforce trademark prosecution on potentially new products approved by other regulatory bodies outside the FDA that the contract references.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For each Active Country, SIGA shall prosecute (a) applications in respect of any SIGA Patents listed on Schedule 1, and (b) trademark registrations for the SIGA Trademarks listed on Schedule 2 (or such other Trademark in respect of the Products as mutually agreed by the Parties at the JSC), in each case ((a) and (b)), with the appropriate Governmental Authorities, provided that there are no Trademarks which may be substantially similar or Patents which may limit patentability, and provided further, if SIGA determines that it is not commercially reasonable to prosecute such Patents and Trademarks, SIGA shall consult with MMT in respect of the appropriate prosecution strategy in such Active Country.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Product' to include both FDA-approved TPOXX and any oral formulation of tecovirimat approved by Regulatory Authorities in the Territory. The changed text narrows this definition to solely FDA-approved TPOXX. The contradicted text, Section 7.3, requires SIGA to pursue trademark registrations for 'Products' with appropriate Governmental Authorities. Because the definition of Product has been narrowed, Section 7.3 now refers to trademark registrations for products approved by Governmental Authorities, which the definition of 'Product' (post-perturbation) now explicitly excludes any non FDA approved regulatory bodies, thus making it contradictory.

perturbation.original_text: MMT may prepare marketing, advertising, promotional materials and other communications relating to the Product for Promotion use in the Field in the Territory. All such materials shall be truthful and non-misleading, and in compliance with applicable Laws, and subject to review by SIGA through the JSC; provided that the final decision to use any approved materials will be at the sole discretion of MMT.
perturbation.changed_text: MMT may prepare marketing, advertising, promotional materials and other communications relating to the Product for Promotion use in the Field in the Territory. All such materials shall be truthful and non-misleading, and in compliance with applicable Laws; provided that the final decision to use any approved materials will be at the sole discretion of MMT.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "and subject to review by SIGA through the JSC", removes a key step in the review process of marketing materials. This omission contradicts Section 3.1, where the Joint Steering Committee (JSC) is established to govern activities related to the promotion of the Product. Removing SIGA's review through the JSC creates an inconsistency: Section 3.1 establishes the JSC's role, yet section 4.1(c) removes the requirement for SIGA's review of promotional materials, undermining the governance structure and creating uncertainity in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Within thirty (30) days after the Effective Date, the Parties will establish a joint steering committee (the "JSC") to govern the activities of the Parties with respect to the Promotion and Commercialization of the Product in the Field in the Territory pursuant to this Agreement. The role of the JSC is: [***]
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for SIGA to review marketing materials through the JSC, as stated in the original text of section 4.1(c). This contradicts the contradicted text, which establishes the JSC's role in governing activities related to the promotion and commercialization of the product. Removing SIGA's review undermines the JSC's authority and creates a direct conflict regarding the review process, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: MMT or its Affiliates will reasonably cooperate with SIGA to meet applicable pharmacovigilance and safety reporting requirements and in the event of a Product recall. To facilitate pharmacovigilance and safety reporting, the Parties agree that they will follow the procedures described in Schedule 3 Safety Reporting Requirements, which may be amended from time-to-time, to ensure that adverse event and other safety information is identified, reviewed, and reported in a manner that will permit SIGA to comply with applicable Laws, including any reporting requirements with any applicable Regulatory Authority.
perturbation.changed_text: MMT or its Affiliates will reasonably cooperate with SIGA to meet applicable pharmacovigilance and safety reporting requirements and in the event of a Product recall. To facilitate pharmacovigilance and safety reporting, the Parties agree to ensure that adverse event and other safety information is identified, reviewed, and reported in a manner that will permit SIGA to comply with applicable Laws, including any reporting requirements with any applicable Regulatory Authority.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "they will follow the procedures described in Schedule 3 Safety Reporting Requirements, which may be amended from time-to-time", it removes a clear directive to adhere to a specific procedure, while still mandating that adverse events be handled appropriately. This introduces an in-text contradiction because without following Schedule 3, there are no defined procedures for pharmacovigilance, thus contradicting the requirement to ensure SIGA can comply with safety reporting to Regulatory Authorities. The obligation remains, but the method for achieving it is removed, leading to an inconsistancy and creating legal ambiguities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: MMT or its Affiliates will reasonably cooperate with SIGA to meet applicable pharmacovigilance and safety reporting requirements and in the event of a Product recall. To facilitate pharmacovigilance and safety reporting, the Parties agree to ensure that adverse event and other safety information is identified, reviewed, and reported in a manner that will permit SIGA to comply with applicable Laws, including any reporting requirements with any applicable Regulatory Authority.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the explicit requirement to follow Schedule 3 for safety reporting, while still requiring that adverse event information be handled appropriately to allow SIGA to comply with applicable laws. Because the contradicted text requires compliance with applicable laws for safety reporting, and the changed text removes the defined procedure for doing so, a contradiction is created. This omission makes it unclear how compliance is to be achieved, leading to a strong inconsistency and legal ambiguity regarding the implementation of pharmacovigilance and safety reporting. Therefore, it is a direct contradiction, according to the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction. The removal of a procedural requirement directly conflicts with the mandated outcome.
